2022-23.equipSource: Over a third of vocational schools lack equipment entirely — students in these schools complete theory without hands-on training, undermining employment readiness.
2022-23.equipNote: 35% of vocational schools have partial equipment — limiting practical learning outcomes. 30% have no equipment at all. From Equipment Status column.
2022-23.sponSource: Centrally Sponsored schools dominate vocational coverage — state-sponsored programs show significant room to scale, particularly in smaller states.
2022-23.sponNote: 58% of vocational schools run under Centrally Sponsored Scheme, 10% State Sponsored, 32% unsupported. From Sponsorship Type column.
2022-23.hoursSource: Theory hours outpace practical training in most programs — a structural imbalance that limits skill depth and reduces placement outcomes for vocational graduates.
2022-23.hoursNote: Total training hours: 0 · 2022-23. Theory: 0%, Practical: 0%, Industry: 0%. Industry Training Hours column captures real-world exposure.
